title: NOC 21223 – Database Analysts and Data Administrators | GTR Immigration
description: Database Analysts and Data Administrators are in strong demand across Canada, supporting digital transformation, cybersecurity readiness, AI platforms, and enterprise-data governance. Classified under NOC 21223 (TEER 1), this occupation qualifies for Express-Entry-linked programs, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) pathways.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.major_group: 212 – Professional occupations in applied sciences (except engineering)

duties: Build and test data models and DBMS platforms
Provide technical guidance on database tools
Operate systems for analytics and data mining
Define security, access, and retention frameworks
Advise IT teams on data availability and compliance
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree or college diploma in computer science, engineering, mathematics, or related field
job_outlook: This creates a favourable immigration environment for overseas candidates with:
additional_information: Canada’s fast-growing tech ecosystem continues to drive hiring for:
Enterprise-data modernizationCloud migrationsAI & analytics platformsCybersecurity governanceFinancial-technology systems
Typical annual earnings range between $44,252–$108,532 CAD, depending on province, experience, and specialization.
